Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-27 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data shows two exceptionally strong performers dominating the short-term momentum rankings. DOW leads at rank 1 with a composite score of 94.1, driven by outstanding ratio consistency (98.5 score) and exceptional resilience during SPY downdays (97.4 score), though its 1-year return of 13.87% lags its shorter-term moves significantly. APA ranks second with even more aggressive returns—108% over one year and 80.34% over six months—and dramatically outperforms major indices on a 1-year basis (95.43% vs SPY), suggesting a strong cyclical or sector-specific tailwind rather than broad market strength. Both tickers pass all four SMA gates and show healthy returns even on bad market days, but APA's ratio consistency score of only 3 passes (versus DOW's 4) indicates slightly less uniform growth acceleration across timeframes. The data reveals a materials and energy sector bias, with both top performers showing highest correlation to XLB (materials) and XLE (energy), suggesting today's momentum is concentrated in commodity-linked trades rather than broad-based strength.
